mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.8 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears and compresses equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the hazard ratio for SPX drawdowns following the threshold breach is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in consumer credit balances indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins and depresses equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression fails to explain more than 2 percent of the variance in forward returns, or the joint signal produces a positive predictive value below 40 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active federal funds rate hikes, a month-over-month decline in housing starts accelerates the repricing of interest rate sensitivity in growth stocks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The negative return predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX term structure, or forward returns conditional on the trigger show no statistically significant difference from the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP above the historical trend rate of 2.0 percent expands corporate revenue expectations and supports positive equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 20-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.15 over any 3-year window, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading that exceeds 3.5 percent annualized triggers a repricing of inflation expectations, forcing higher discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns following the threshold breach are not statistically lower than baseline returns, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50 percent over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth accelerates while real GDP growth decelerates indicates an unsustainable productivity drag that will force corporate margin compression and generate negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The cross-sectional predictive power of the employment-GDP divergence fails to exceed a Rank IC of 0.02 over any rolling 3-year period, or forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production peaks and consumer credit simultaneously contracts, supply chain bottlenecks amplify cost-push inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change falls below 0.2 percent
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict drawdowns with a hit rate above 55 percent, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for credit spread movements.
